rationale.D1: Drivetrain extends SN_SuperSwerveV2 (vendored swerve abstraction via SuperCORE library), but mechanisms like Rotors and Motion own TalonFX hardware objects directly with no IO interface or swappable implementation — vendored abstraction for drive only, mechanisms hardware-welded.
rationale.D2: StateMachine.tryState() implements a real centralized FSM with guarded switch-within-switch transitions across 18+ RobotState enum values, plus a separate DriverStateMachine; lacks a full Superstructure goal-fanout API (no kinematic planner, no requested-vs-actual separation), placing it between D2 and D3.
rationale.D3: No simulationPeriodic, no WPILib physics classes (ElevatorSim, FlywheelSim, DCMotorSim), and no maple-sim found anywhere in the source; CI runs simulateJava for build-verification only, not physics-backed mechanism simulation.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory exists and no @Test annotations found; CI runs ./gradlew build (not test), so there are zero unit tests to run.
rationale.D5: Epilogue @Logged annotation applied to Robot, RobotContainer, and all subsystems (Rotors, Motion, Vision, Drivetrain, StateMachine, DriverStateMachine, RobotPoses, Telemetry); Epilogue.bind(this) called in robotInit plus DataLogManager and custom logger classes — systematic structured logging throughout but not AdvantageKit processInputs.
rationale.D6: Choreo trajectories are fully wired: ChoreoTraj auto-generated constants reference 40+ .chor trajectories, AutoFactory.trajectoryCmd drives them in 20+ named competition autos in configAutonomous(), and followTrajectory handles trajectory following with PID correction — Choreo adoption is real and extensive.
rationale.D7: Three Limelights with MegaTag2, per-camera rejection logic (gyro rate, tag count, area threshold), multi-camera fusion selecting by tag count, and addVisionMeasurement with timestamp called in a dedicated command — confirmed pose fusion; no TimeInterpolatableBuffer and vision is not behind an IO interface.
rationale.D8: Five seasons of repos all have CI, SuperCORE library is versioned with a create-release workflow and actively consumed as a dependency in 2026 code (SN_SuperSwerveV2, SN_XboxController imports confirmed), plus Standard_Swerve/Elevator/Turret training templates — program-grade library infrastructure.
